title: Chapter Eighty-Six: The Blood Robe
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

Ten seconds.

That was the number he gave Namgung Hwa-sin when he first spotted Namgung Mi across the courtyard. Ten seconds to subdue the Flourishing Spirit Flower Master in formal combat.

But the situation had changed.

Namgung Mi had seized Wuchang's public sentiment like a woman plucking fruit from a low branch -- effortlessly, with a smile that said she had been doing it all her life. She had framed Virtuous Teacher Sect's servants as evil sect practitioners, destroyed their dantians before a crowd, and turned the whole affair into a performance of righteous justice. The people loved her for it. The local martial artists fell over themselves to praise her beauty, her martial prowess, her *benevolence*.

Desolate Sect warriors could not kill recklessly in life-and-death matches when the crowd already saw them as oppressors.

*So we change the stage.*

"Mercy in methods..." Namgung Hwa-sin trailed off, staring at the crimson bundle in Yeon-shin's hands. The pure blood robe. The demon mask.

"However, the order has changed," Hwa-sin said, lifting his head. Sunlight carved along his sharp features -- the face that earned him the title White Qilin, handsome enough to make men pause. But his eyes held something fragile. "Namgung Mi is a direct-line rising talent. She consumed a peerless spirit medicine -- Human-Shaped Snow Ginseng. Her yin cold energy freezes meridians on contact. It dulls the body, slows true qi circulation, saps explosive strength. The longer the fight, the worse it becomes. Compatibility before martial arts level."

"I know."

"I'm not dismissing you. I mean there are martial arts hard to counter even at equal rank." Hwa-sin's hands trembled. He clasped them together. "Starting with me. I volunteered for this mission to overcome heart demons. I'm unsure I can overcome them in time."

Yeon-shin watched those trembling hands. This mission carried weight for both of them -- the main fortress needed governance over the murim, Yeon-shin needed merit to survive, and Hwa-sin needed to face the family that had tormented his mother and cast him out like refuse.

*During the competition he tried to defeat me so he could fight the young master personally. Does he carry a grudge against Azure Qilin too?*

"Though I can't lift your heart up--" Yeon-shin began.

"Haha." A small, brittle laugh. "I don't expect such things from you, Young Hero Jeong."

"It seems I can pull Namgung Mi down."

The laugh died. Hwa-sin stared at him.

Yeon-shin held up the demon mask. A gaping dragon's mouth, frozen mid-snarl.

---

Namgung Mi bit into a peach.

She sat in Virtuous Teacher Sect's central hall as if it were a throne room, draped across a chair covered in tiger skin, one hand resting on each armrest. She had the bearing of a monarch. The snow-white skin. The unhurried way she chewed, lips barely moving, as though even eating were beneath her.

"Do it," she said.

For the attendants kneeling before her, the words were a death sentence.

The Flourishing Spirit Flower warriors moved forward. Internal energy flared in their palms. Their eyes held nothing -- not cruelty, not pity. Simply indifference. The attendants were not equals. They were not even people.

"Save us! Please save us!"

"I was dragged here!"

"I won't kill you," Namgung Mi said, and smiled. The smile had dignity in it, which made it worse. "I'll just destroy your dantians. You learned vicious evil sect techniques. Since you served this wicked sect, who knows what you might do to commoners?"

"Never learned martial arts -- *HEOK!*"

Screams erupted. The warriors' palms found the Ocean of Qi points, and blood vessels ruptured with sounds like wet cloth tearing. Over a dozen attendants writhed on the floor. Blood came from below and from their mouths simultaneously. The hall's stone floor turned red.

Namgung Mi reached for another peach.

"Clean up and call the people," she said. "I'll hold a feast distributing this evil sect's wealth."

---

Sunset stretched the shadows long across Virtuous Teacher Sect's courtyard. The practice ground had become a banquet hall. Local dignitaries -- escort agency heads, martial hall masters, merchant leaders, even the government school chancellor -- sat around a massive table dragged from inside, eating and drinking and praising Namgung Mi as if she had descended from heaven itself.

"A snow mountain fairy," one man sighed.

"Truly thankful," said another.

Namgung Mi tilted her head. Her glossy hair shifted sideways -- an intentional movement, calculated to the last strand.

"I wonder why Desolate Sect left Virtuous Teacher Sect alone?" she said. "When so many commoners were suffering."

The crowd understood what she wanted. They gave it to her eagerly.

"They're just desperate to strangle the murim--"

"Desolate Sect sent life-and-death challenge letters to Namgung who fought fierce battles against Blood Flame Sect. They drop murim principles to the ground--"

*The careful planner is the strong one in this world.* Namgung Mi smiled and reached for a peach at the edge of the table.

"Who."

Every voice stopped. Every hand froze. The single word carried weight that pressed against the chest like a stone.

"Spoke of our sect."

The voice was young but threaded with the rupturing harmonics of true qi channelled through the Heaven's Edge point above the vocal cords. A boyish tone wrapped in something ancient and terrible.

A red figure crossed the wall. Bright crimson against the orange sky -- a blood robe trailing behind like a wound in the air. The body technique was flawless. Not a sound. Not a speck of dust.

STEP.

Small footsteps as the figure landed in the banquet hall. A demon mask grinned at them all -- a dragon's mouth torn wide, frozen in a rictus of amusement.

Energy waves rippled outward from the robed figure like wind across water. Delicate. Controlled. The kind of power that didn't need to announce itself because it was *already there*, pressing against the skin of everyone present.

"Blood... Flame Sect?" someone whispered.

Namgung Mi deployed the Azure Sky Eye Technique. Sky-coloured radiance filled her irises as she swept her gaze from the intruder's head to feet, searching for any tremor, any weakness.

She found none.

The demon mask spoke again.

"You." A pause. "Are you the Namgung bastard?"

No one opened their mouths. The banquet had become a tomb.

---
